2nd Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment; Request For Temporary Stay Of Enforcement; Request For Short Continuance Or, In The Alternative, Leave To Supplement, Pending Related Disciplinary/Agency Investigations; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Andrew Arthur Peterson; [Proposed]Order
Off Calendar Dec-09-2025 Continued To Jan-06-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ANDREW PETERSON 2nd Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment; Request For Temporary Stay Of Enforcement; Request For Short Continuance Or, In The Alternative, Leave To Supplement, Pending Related Disciplinary/Agency Investigations; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Andrew Arthur Peterson; [Proposed]Order

Appearances:
Andrew A. Peterson, in person, Defendant in propria persona.
Gary Bemis, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, Legal Dept. of GFCS, Inc., (800) 646-4237, for Plaintiff.

Not Argued/Contested. At the outset, the court recognizes the notice issues regarding the two subject matters set for hearing today and of Defendants request to continue the subject matters. Counsel for Plaintiff objects to the continuance. The court advises that it intends to grant a brief continuance, and a mutually agreeable date is discussed and confirmed.
Defendant points to a declaration filed by Plaintiff (the "Efrain Vasquez" declaration) which includes his social security number and additional information which he believes should not be available to the public via the court's docket and asks that the court consider sealing the document. The preceding issue is discussed. The court requests that Plaintiff not include any sensitive information in any future filings. Upon consideration, the court advises that it intends to seal the previously mentioned declaration as requested and the court intends to issue an order thereon. Counsel for Plaintiff is instructed to file an updated declaration that redacts the information by rule of court 1.201 within three (3) weeks of entry of the court's order.

The subject matter is continued to Jan-06-2026 at 9:00 AM in Dept. 301 as confirmed in open court.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

*Amended* Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment; Request For Temporary Stay Of Enforcement; Request For Short Continuance Or, In The Alternative, Leave To Supplement, Pending Related Disciplinary/Agency Investigations; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Andrew Arthur Peterson
Off Calendar Dec-09-2025 Continued To Jan-06-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ANDREW PETERSON *Amended* Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment; Request For Temporary Stay Of Enforcement; Request For Short Continuance Or, In The Alternative, Leave To Supplement, Pending Related Disciplinary/Agency Investigations; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Andrew Arthur Peterson.

Appearances:

Andrew A. Peterson, in person, Defendant in propria persona.

Gary Bemis, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, Legal Dept. of GFCS, Inc., (800) 646-4237, for Plaintiff.

Not Argued/Contested. At the outset, the court recognizes the notice issues regarding the two subject matters set for hearing today and of Defendants request to continue the subject matters and of the Temporary Restraining order. Counsel for Plaintiff objects to the continuance. The court advises that it intends to grant a brief continuance, and a mutually agreeable date is discussed and confirmed.
Defendant points to a declaration filed by Plaintiff (the "Efrain Vasquez" declaration) which includes his social security number and additional information which he believes should not be available to the public via the court's docket and asks that the court consider sealing the document. The preceding issue is discussed. The court requests that Plaintiff not include any sensitive information in any future filings. Upon consideration, the court advises that it intends to seal the previously mentioned declaration as requested and the court intends to issue an order thereon. Counsel for Plaintiff is instructed to file an updated declaration that redacts the information by rule of court 1.201 within three (3) weeks of entry of the court's order.

The subject matter is continued to Jan-06-2026 at 9:00 AM in Dept. 301 as confirmed in open court.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)
